title: kibessia tuberculata
side_effects: Kibessia tuberculata can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe allergic reactions, skin irritation, and inflammation, particularly when ingested or applied topically without proper dilution. In rare cases, prolonged use can cause stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and even kidney damage due to its high concentration of alkaloids. Excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may also lead to dizziness, nausea, and respiratory issues, including bronchitis and asthma attacks. Moreover, improper preparation and handling can cause eye irritation, skin burns, and even long-term damage to the liver and kidneys.
